subject.title: Biology
subject.description:
**IB Biology** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **four interconnected themes: Unity and Diversity, Form and Function, Interaction and Interdependence, and Continuity and Change**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, scientific inquiry, data analysis, and practical investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice and data-based questions | 1 hour 20 min | 2 hours | 36% |
| **Paper 2** | Short and extended response questions | 1 hour 30 min | 2 hours 30 min | 44% |
| **Scientific Investigation** | Individual research project | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: The research question is framed within a specific biological and real-world context, linking Vigna radiata, discrete wavelength ranges and shoot length.
Key variables are clearly identified and control measures (light intensity, temperature, water volume, species choice) are outlined.
weaknesses: Numerical justification for chosen wavelength bands and quantitative control parameters (lux or Î¼molâmâ»Â²âsâ»Â¹ measurements, lamp distance) are omitted, limiting methodological rigor.
There is no rationale or calculation supporting sampleâsize sufficiency for statistical validity.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The research question context is strong and specific (band 5), but methodological considerations and description, while adequate, contain notable omissions (quantitative controls, sample size). This places the work solidly in the middle of the 3â4 band (holistic 3).

strengths: Data tables and graphs are clearly labelled; units and significant figures are precise throughout.
Data processing (means, standard deviations, one-way ANOVA) is correctly carried out and reported, matching raw data.
weaknesses: Consideration of measurement and biological uncertainties is minimalâno instrument error margins or propagation in statistical tests.
Statistical reporting is incomplete (empty SUMMARY table, inconsistent wavelength category definitions), reducing analytical completeness.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Communication and processing of data are strong and mostly accurate (bands 5â6), but significant omissions in uncertainty analysis and some inconsistencies place the criterion at a mid-band performance (holistic 3).

strengths: The conclusion is relevant to the research question, logically consistent with the ANOVA results and justified by the analysis.
There is a connection to the accepted scientific context (action spectra, literature on light effects).
weaknesses: Comparison to scientific context is superficialâno quantitative literature values are contrasted.
Repetition of background details in the conclusion reduces focus and conciseness.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion is relevant and supported by data (band 5 in relevance) but the depth of scientific context comparison is limited (band 3). Overall this aligns with a lower mid-band performance (holistic 2).

strengths: Specific methodological weaknesses (cellophane spectral purity, lamp heating, short duration) are identified and their impacts are clearly explained.
Improvements proposed (switch to LEDs, extend duration) are realistic, relevant and explained in terms of how they address the limitations.
weaknesses: The evaluation omits discussion of sample-size limitations and uncertainty measurement improvements.
Some improvement explanations lack detail on how extended duration would affect variance or long-term developmental observations.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student offers detailed, specific weaknesses and explains their relative impact (band 5â6) and proposes well-justified improvements (band 5â6). Minor omissions prevent a top score, resulting in a high-mid performance (holistic 4).

strandSelections.reasoning: The student states that natural light produced the greatest mean stem length and explicitly links this to the ANOVA P-value, thereby **justifying the conclusion with the presented analysis**.  Logical consistency is maintained throughout the narrative.  Minor stylistic repetition prevents a perfect 6, but the strand fits solidly within the top band, so 5 is awarded.

Raw data are organised into clearly headed tables with units; processed means, SDs and ANOVA outputs are tabulated; a column graph with error bars is provided and axes are fully labelled.  This communication is **both clear (readable, correctly labelled) and precise (units, significant figures consistently to 2 d.p.)**.  Minor layout issues (empty âSUMMARYâ header row) prevent a perfect 6, but overall performance securely meets the 5â6 band.

Means, standard deviations and a one-way ANOVA are all correctly executed and reported.  Processed values are internally consistent with the raw data, and error bars match SD calculations.  While the student does not show the intermediate formulae, the **processing is appropriate and accurate** for answering the question, comfortably achieving the 5â6 band; a 5 is assigned because checks of normality or post-hoc tests are not included.

Specific weaknesses are identified (cellophane spectral purity, lamp heating, short duration) and the student **explains the impact** of each on data accuracy, reliability or scope.  While discussion is concise, the causal links are explicit, satisfying the 5â6 band.  Depth is good but not exhaustive (e.g., sample size not critiqued), therefore a 5 is appropriate.

The conclusion is compared to accepted scientific ideasâabsorption/action spectra, literature on red and blue light effectsâand citations are provided.  However, the comparison is **somewhat superficial**: no quantitative literature values are contrasted, and the discussion mostly reiterates textbook statements.  This meets the 3â4 descriptor; a mid-band score of 3 reflects limited depth.

The research question clearly specifies the organism (*Vigna radiata*), the quantitative independent variable (discrete wavelength ranges) and the dependent variable (shoot length over one week).  The introduction and background paragraph link the question to photosynthetic pigments, agricultural applications and the practical choice of a fast-germinating legume, establishing a **specific biological and real-world context** rather than a generic rationale.  While the context could have been strengthened with numerical justification of wavelength bands, what is provided goes well beyond a mere statement, fitting the 5â6 band; a mid-band score of 5 is appropriate because the context, although specific, is not exceptionally detailed.

Uncertainty is considered through the calculation of standard deviation and display of error bars, satisfying basic expectations.  However, **instrumental uncertainties (Â±0.1 cm ruler), biological variation explanations, and propagation in the ANOVA test are absent**, and SD is the only metric used.  These omissions correspond to âsome significant omissionsâ, warranting a low 3â4 band mark of 3.

For every weakness raised, a **realistic and relevant improvement** is offeredâreplacing cellophane with calibrated LEDs, extending experiment to three weeks.  These improvements are justified in terms of enhancing wavelength accuracy and capturing longer-term growth variables, demonstrating explanation rather than mere listing.  Minor omissions (e.g., adding light meters) keep the score at 5 rather than 6.

The student identifies all major methodological issues (light intensity, temperature, water volume, duration, seed species) and states how each will be controlled.  This demonstrates a **clear description** of considerations needed to obtain relevant and sufficient data.  However, the justification is uneven: there is no discussion of sample size sufficiency, distance of lamp to seeds, or quantitative monitoring of light intensity, so the explanation lacks depth.  Hence the strand fulfils the 3â4 band with the stronger end (4) merited by the breadth but not depth of discussion.

Step-by-step instructions, labelled materials tables and control measures would enable another investigator to reproduce the core procedure.  Nevertheless, key details such as lamp wattage/lux, exact distance between lamp and seedlings, and the precise method for measuring stem length (from cotyledon or soil surface) are omitted, leaving **minor ambiguities**.  This aligns with the 3â4 descriptor; a top-band (5â6) award is not justified.

annotations.comment: Table 1 on variables is missing content in the cells for independent/dependent/controlled variables, making it ambiguous and unreproducible.
The absorption and action spectra discussion is thorough; however, include a citation for the Passel diagram within the text to support accuracy.
The control of light intensity relies solely on using the same brand of bulb; consider measuring and reporting light intensity in lux or Î¼molâmâ»Â²âsâ»Â¹ for rigor.
The methodology omits the distance between lamp and seedlingsâa critical parameter for light dosage. Specify exact height to allow reproducibility.
Why is the water initially heated for germination? Clarify the rationale to justify its effect on seed viability.
The header row in Table 4 is blank for the first columnâadd a descriptive label (e.g., âConditionâ) to improve clarity and data communication.
Table 5 presents only red light statistics; means and standard deviations for the other wavelengths are missing, reducing completeness of analysis.
Wavelength categories in the summary table are inconsistent (e.g., 450â490 nm vs. earlier 450â495 nm). Harmonize definitions to avoid confusion.
Graph 1 lacks a legend clarifying that error bars represent standard deviation. Add annotation or caption detail to specify the uncertainty metric.
The research question is described within a specific biological and real-world context, clearly linking the plant species, discrete wavelength ranges, and shoot length measure over a week.

While the introduction outlines key concepts, the discussion lacks justification of sample size and quantitative control parameters (e.g., lamp distance, light intensity), reducing depth of methodological explanation.
Consider specifying numerical justification for chosen wavelength ranges (e.g., citing spectral peak values) to strengthen the rationale and demonstrate targeted selection of bands.
The narrative around Figure 1 reiterates background without adding new insightâcould clarify how the image supports the experimental context or remove redundancy.
The background provides a clear species choice and defines variables well, contextualizing the study within agricultural applications.
Include an estimate of instrumental uncertainty (e.g., Â±0.1âcm for the ruler) and discuss biological variation to deepen consideration of measurement uncertainties.
The null and alternative hypotheses are clearly stated, but Hâ would be stronger if it specified the expected direction or magnitude of change under different wavelengths.
Risk assessment addresses lamp hazard only; include controls for water spillage and electrical safety to ensure comprehensive hazard management.
The ANOVA summary (âSUMMARYâ table) is empty with no group data; populate count, sum, average, and variance to complete statistical reporting.
Discuss checks of ANOVA assumptions (normality, homogeneity of variance) to justify the validity of the F-test and strengthen the analysis.
The ANOVA table correctly reports SS, df, MS, F, P-value, and Fâcritâ, providing an accurate and appropriate statistical summary.
The limitation regarding lamp heating and cellophane fading is specific and the impact on spectral purity is clearly explained.
Extending the experiment beyond one week is a realistic improvement; consider explaining how longer duration could reveal trends in leaf or flower development and affect variance.
Switching to LED lights is a well-justified, realistic improvement that directly addresses the spectral overlap issue introduced by cellophane.
The discussion of basil and LED applications shows strong real-world relevance, linking experimental findings to agricultural practice.
The conclusion repeats background details and could be more concise: focus on synthesizing results with analysis rather than reintroducing context.
Enhance the conclusion by comparing your measured stem elongation rates quantitatively with literature values or action spectra data for strengthened scientific context.
The works cited list is inconsistent in formattingâadopt a uniform citation style (e.g., APA or MLA) for author names, dates, and titles.

The research question is stated without context.
Methodological considerations associated with collecting data relevant to the research question are stated.
The description of the methodology for collecting or selecting data lacks the detail to allow for the investigation to be reproduced.
The description of the methodology for collecting or selecting data allows for the investigation to be reproduced with few ambiguities or omissions.
The research question is outlined within a broad context.
Methodological considerations associated with collecting relevant and sufficient data to answer the research question are described.
The research question is described within a specific and appropriate context.
Methodological considerations associated with collecting relevant and sufficient data to answer the research question are explained.
The description of the methodology for collecting or selecting data allows for the investigation to be reproduced.
No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.
No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.
No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.
The recording and processing of the data is communicated but is neither clear nor precise.
Some processing of data relevant to addressing the research question is carried out but with major omissions, inaccuracies or inconsistencies.
The recording and processing of data shows limited evidence of the consideration of uncertainties.
The processing of data relevant to addressing the research question is carried out but with some significant omissions, inaccuracies or inconsistencies.
The communication of the recording and processing of the data is either clear or precise.
The recording and processing of data shows evidence of a consideration of uncertainties but with some significant omissions or inaccuracies.
The processing of data relevant to addressing the research question is carried out appropriately and accurately.
The recording and processing of data shows evidence of an appropriate consideration of uncertainties.
The communication of the recording and processing of the data is both clear and precise.

rubricCriterionStrands.name: Communication of data recording and processing

No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.
The conclusion makes superficial comparison to the accepted scientific context.
A conclusion is stated that is relevant to the research question but is not supported by the analysis presented.
A conclusion is described that makes some relevant comparison to the accepted scientific context.
A conclusion is described that is relevant to the research question but is not fully consistent with the analysis presented.
A conclusion is justified that is relevant to the research question and fully consistent with the analysis presented.
A conclusion is justified through relevant comparison to the accepted scientific context.
No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.
No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.
Realistic improvements to the investigation are stated.
The report states generic methodological weaknesses or limitations.
Realistic improvements to the investigation that are relevant to the identified weaknesses or limitations, are described.
The report describes specific methodological weaknesses or limitations.
Realistic improvements to the investigation, that are relevant to the identified weaknesses or limitations, are explained.
The report explains the relative impact of specific methodological weaknesses or limitations.
